{{Quote|They came from the Galactic Empire's side of the border, but they're not friends of the Empire. They call themselves the Rebel Alliance and they seek to embroil us in their struggle to overthrow Emperor Palpatine. Speaking as the leader of my clan, I oppose the Airam Council's decision to ally with the Rebels. We have little to gain and much to lose in joining a war against the Empire. The Rebels are weak and in retreat; their victories have been minor and chiefly symbolic. We long ago learned to avoid Imperial warships because of a few unpleasant incidents, and this has been an easy thing to do because they have so rarely entered our territory. Yet now we know they will come looking for the Rebels. And we know how they deal with those who aid the Rebels. We owe our success to two things: Isolation, independence and utter ruthlessness. Oh, three things!|Ilay|<ref name="XT">''[[Star Wars: X-Wing vs. TIE Fighter: Balance of Power Campaigns]]''</ref>}}
'''Ilay''' was a clan leader of the [[Airam (group)|Airam]]. Unlike most of her rival clan leaders, Ilay believed that joining the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] was a serious mistake, and instead remain with the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].

She eventually met with [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Wooyou Senn|Senn's]] forces in her [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[Magua]]'' in the [[Aiqin 4 system]] despite being pursued by rebel forces, which were driven off by Imperial forces. Admiral Senn promised her that she would be [[Governor/Legends|governor]] of the entire [[Airam sector/Legends|Airam sector]], should the Alliance be driven out. In return, she leaked information about a planned exchange of rebel weapons for Airam [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]] near [[Elbra]]. This subsequently led to the capture of the rebel [[GR-75 medium transport/Legends|Medium Transport]] ''[[Hoopaju]]'' (carrying weapons) and the Airam [[Modular conveyor]] ''[[Pobel]]'' by forces from [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|ISD]] [[Rage (Imperial-class)|''Rage'']] while the escorting Airam [[Muurian transport]] [[Lod (ship)|''Lod'']] was destroyed. She also revealed the location of an upcoming rebel/Airam resupply operation in the [[The Drift/Legends|Kuras Drift]] and even went so far to send her own forces to assist the Empire in an (albeit unsuccessful) [[Mission to Kuras Drift|strike mission]] against that resupply operation.

After the battle in the Kuras Drift, Ilay came for another meeting to keep her settlement with the Empire. This time she traveled to [[Aiqin 4]] aboard the [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|''Dreadnaught''-class heavy cruiser]] [[Kala'din (starship)|''Kala'din'']]. The Rebels had learned of this meeting, however, and managed to kidnap her in a [[Battle of Aiqin 4|surprise attack]]. Through interrogation it was then revealed that she had sold conscripts and warheads to the Empire. The Rebels [[Battle of Dega|stopped]] the attempt to resupply the Imperials. The Airam conscripts later confirmed that they were to undergo basic flight training aboard the Star Dreadnaught and then sent on suicide missions against the Alliance forces in the sector. The large supply of warheads provided by Ilay would have proven lethal to the rebels in such missions.

The [[Battle of Nocto]] that concluded the [[Imperial Task Force Vengeance|Imperial campaign]] against the Alliance and the Airam rebels soon afterward.
